required_architecture_or_doctrine: LifeOS is the living company, personal, development, and home operating environment; David remains final authority.
Human intent becomes a governed, simulated, executable, verified task graph ending in a proven next-action recommendation.
Every meaningful action passes through identity, policy, memory, simulation, execution, proof, and rollback; no agent receives unlimited authority.
All valid peer capabilities converge physically and upgrade-only into one grown-up LifeOS application rather than remaining a permanently orchestrated fleet.
The consolidated application is Rust-native, uses governed Lua policy surfaces, and runs on the portable confined Yazelix plus Nix foundation.
The first self-sustaining product vertical is governed financial and legal operation.
The first build is the governed task-execution substrate required by every later LifeOS vertical.
The shared task contract, witnessed proof authority, execution kernel, runtime integration, and mandatory human-approval gates converge without parallel competing types.
Company, development, personal, and home capabilities become governed LifeOS domains over the same consolidated substrate.
Memory, failure learning, local inference and training, mesh compute and storage, communications, regenerative configuration, secrets, and network control form one governed intelligence substrate.
Desktop and later approved mobile, glasses, and ambient interfaces expose the same LifeOS state while the replaceable UI remains subordinate to the Rust-native substrate.
Scope boundaries, doctrine, proof-based success, anti-drift, rollback, Ubuntu-first release, and continuous governed improvement remain enforceable end to end.

PostgreSQL plus RuVector is the target canonical editable macro-state substrate for the complete LifeOS user-space, but the exact relationship to valid current redb, nu_plugin CodeDB, AgentDB, envctl migration, secret-store, and projection use cases must be owner-decided without capability loss or silent dual authority.
phase_task_ids: LIFEOS-002; POSTGRES-001; PGAUTH-002; PGAUTH-006; ADOPT-001; ADOPT-002; STORE-001; POSTGRES-006

required_architecture_or_doctrine: The entire user-space, including planning and control tables, is represented by stable relational, temporal, semantic, provenance, permission, history, blob-reference, encrypted-secret, and graph identities; every planning question is queried and every accepted answer is updated in PostgreSQL.

Archive-first collectors ingest every user-space entity losslessly through restartable staging; RuVector adds reproducible embeddings, hybrid retrieval, semantic SQL edits, copy-on-write branches, temporal alternatives, witness chains, and MinCut isolation.

LifeOS Mission Control reads and directs canonical database state through a local UDS contract and redb-backed low-latency views; no HTTP or editable flat-file detour becomes a parallel control plane.

envctl deterministically projects a selected database branch and version into a disposable directory; Yazelix plus Nix consumes that projection to produce portable, reproducible LifeOS builds and executable closures.

Flat files cease to be editable authority only after whole-user-space parity, behavioral equivalence, deterministic reconstruction, backup, restore, and failback pass; preserved history remains available and projections remain traceable to database identities.

Valid existing work is an upgrade input, not a reason to rebuild: contradictory prior task tables are reconciled against source and proof; proven CodeDB, envctl migration, secret, PostgreSQL, RuVector, redb, and materialization capabilities are adopted once; unresolved store relationships remain explicit decisions.
phase_task_ids: GRAPH-002; RECOVERY-001; RECOVERY-002; RECOVERY-003; RECOVERY-004; ADOPT-001; ADOPT-002; STORE-001

required_architecture_or_doctrine: NotebookLM architecture objects are processed one at a time into atomic claims; object provenance, engine-versus-application ownership, task coverage, contradictions, unresolved verification, and proof are complete before the next object is allowed to influence architecture. Every unresolved claim enters a stable verification queue; later sources remain linked evidence rather than blended truth; only primary evidence, reproducible benchmarks, or explicit owner decisions close claims.
